attributes.description: Alerts when the p99 read latency exceeds 100 ms (`cassandra.client.request.read.latency.99p > 100000 us`). This is the primary read-side SLI for Cassandra.
attributes.name: [Cassandra OTel] High read latency (p99)

investigation_guide.blob:
## Cassandra high read latency (p99)

### What fired
`cassandra.client.request.read.latency.99p` exceeded 100 ms on at least one node during the evaluation window.

### Why it matters
Read latency p99 is the user-facing latency for point-lookup reads. Sustained high p99 reflects disk pressure, compaction backlog, GC pauses, or network issues between coordinator and replicas. Client SLAs break long before p99 becomes extreme.

### Triage
1. Overview dashboard: identify affected nodes and correlate with compaction pending, hints-in-progress, and error rate.
2. Check disk I/O utilisation and free page cache.
3. GC pauses — large young-gen or old-gen GCs pause the JVM and directly impact p99.
4. Inspect per-query patterns — hot partitions can dominate a node.

### Remediation
- Resolve compaction backlog.
- Fix hot partitions (data modelling).
- Add capacity or tune JVM/GC for lower pauses.

### Tuning
- 100 ms is a reasonable default for point lookups. Tune to the consuming application's SLA.

esqlQuery.esql:
TS metrics-cassandra.otel-default
// p99 read latency in microseconds; backtick-quote field names with numeric segments
| WHERE `cassandra.client.request.read.latency.99p` IS NOT NULL
| STATS p99_us = MAX(MAX_OVER_TIME(`cassandra.client.request.read.latency.99p`)) BY server.address
// Threshold: 100000 us = 100 ms; tune for your SLA
| WHERE p99_us > 100000
| SORT p99_us DESC
